title: Add cross-device cloud save to a mobile roguelike

facts.text: Game is a Unity 2D roguelike currently using local-only PlayerPrefs/JSON saves.
Cloud save must be built on PlayFab, which is already integrated for leaderboards.
Must support 3 named save slots per player.
Saves must work offline and sync automatically when connectivity returns.
Would like a manual 'sync now' button in the pause menu.
Conflicting offline saves to the same slot on two devices must be resolved without silently losing progress.
Would like a 'last synced' timestamp shown in the save-slot UI.
Would like each save file kept under 50KB.
Existing local saves must be migrated to the cloud system on first launch post-update with zero data loss.
Would like the save file to be human-readable JSON.

ambiguities: Which device wins by default when a save conflict is detected -- most recent timestamp, most progress, or always ask the player.
Whether save slots can be renamed by the player or are fixed labels.
Whether cloud save requires a PlayFab account/login or works anonymously tied to a device ID.
The offline duration threshold before a sync conflict is even flagged versus silently overwritten.
latent: The minimum supported OS versions (iOS 13 / Android 8) require pinning to a specific PlayFab offline SDK version due to known cache issues.
There is an App Store featured-placement submission deadline in 3 weeks, so migration risk needs a rollback plan that does not require a forced app update.
